title: Reinforcing Sanctions on Iranian Terrorists Act
short_title: Reinforcing Sanctions on Iranian Terrorists Act
plain_summary: This bill makes the President decide within three months whether to punish Iran Airports Company with sanctions. Congress believes that this company helps an Iranian airline called Mahan Air do bad things. Mahan Air supports terrorism and helps Iran's military by flying fighters to Syria on the same planes that carry regular passengers to other countries. The bill is meant to stop Iran from using its airports and airlines to support dangerous activities.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Reinforcing Sanctions on Iranian Terrorists Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Application of Sanctions Regarding Mahan Air
sections.text: Congress has found the following facts. First, on October twelfth, two thousand eleven, Mahan Air was added to a special government list that freezes the money and property of people and groups that support terrorism. This list is kept by the Treasury Department. The list stops American people and businesses from doing business with Mahan Air. Second, on March twenty-fourth, two thousand sixteen, the Treasury Department announced more punishment against Mahan Air. The Treasury said that Mahan Air is based in Tehran, Iran and has helped Iran's military force called the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps Qods Force move people, weapons, and money. Mahan Air also supports Iran's harmful actions in the region by flying planes to Syria to transport fighters. Mahan Air uses the same planes that go to Syria to also fly regular passenger trips to places in Europe, the Middle East, and Asia. Third, Mahan Air reportedly flies to these airports in Iran: Abadan Airport, Ahvaz International Airport, Ardabil Airport, Persian Gulf Airport, Bandar Abbas International Airport, Birjand International Airport, Bojnord International Airport, Iranshahr Airport, Isfahan International Airport, Kalaleh Airport, Kerman Airport, Shahid Ashrafi Esffahani Airport, Khorramabad Airport, Kish Airport, Larestan International Airport, Mashhad International Airport, Queshm International Airport, Sahahdaj Airport, Dasht-e Naz Airport, Shiraz International Airport, Sirjan Airport, Tabriz International Airport, Tehran Imam Khomeini International Airport, Mehrabad International Airport, Zabol Airport, Zahedan International Airport, and Zanjan Airport. Fourth, the Iran Airports Company is owned by Iran's government and runs the civilian airports in Iran. This company helps Mahan Air operate. Fifth, foreign people or groups that help, sponsor, or give money, materials, technology, or other services to support Mahan Air should face American sanctions under a presidential order that targets terrorists. Within ninety days after this law is passed, the President must send a decision to the appropriate committees in Congress. The President must decide whether to apply sanctions against the Iran Airports Company under the presidential order that targets terrorists. The President can apply all or some of the available sanctions. The President's decision must be sent in a form that is not classified as secret, but it can include a secret section if needed.
